I’d love to be that
I sent this to my boyfriend about his asshole cat and that very night Pickles (asshole cat) had to go to the emergency vet and now we all have to be nice to him because he almost died even though it cost $4,100

item.dc:creator: UncleMartha

item.title: I eat my veggies
item.description: I was at the grocery store, walked up to the zucchini pile and this old lady walks up as I picked up one. She then releases a shrill raspy accusation that I’ve taken the one and only zucchini she wanted from the pile and needs it! I was sorta stunned at her arrogance and entitlement. All I could say was ‘Thank you for allowing me to ruin your day’ and I walked away. Old people can be the worst.
item.dc:creator: smegheadenergy

item.title: Be the thorn.
item.description: Remember, You don't know any trans people, You don't know any gay people, You don't know any immigrants, You don't know anyone who is pregnant, You don't know anyone who has had a abortion, You don't know any who is Jewish, You don't know anyone who is Muslim, You don't know anyone who is atheist, You didn't see anything, You didn't hear anything, You don't know anything, A cop asks you a question You say I don't speak to Police, They try and stop you, You ask am I being detained? If they say no, walk away. If they say yes, say I will not speak with out a lawyer, I request a lawyer. Say it exactly like that. The police and the government are a hostile force and cannot be trusted. If you can wipe everything off FB, twitter, Instagram and email their support teams and tell them to delete your data from their systems. Every election you register for, sheriff, mayor, governor, no matter how small. Register and vote. Our job for the next 4 years is to be the biggest legal thorn in their side. Flood your rep with emails, letters and calls. Be the thorn.
item.dc:creator: thetinymonarch

item.title: A Museum in Netherland
item.description: In the Netherlands, there is a museum that shows you the entire human body from the inside. You become a tiny person inside the recreation of a human body. It is located in the Corpus Museum in Oegstgeest.

item.dc:creator: speroni

item.title: The Story Behind The Shot: ‘Blade Runner’ and the masterful ‘Tears of rain’ monologue
item.description: Although the 42-word monologue was written by David Peoples, the late Dutch actor Rutger Hauer made some considerable alterations that made the moment truly iconic. In fact, Hauer went against a portion of People’s work, cutting it short and replacing it with the influential line, “All those moments will be lost in time, like tears in rain”. The actor later described the original monologue as “opera talk” and “hi-tech speech” that had no relevance to the rest of the film in the Channel 4 documentary On the Edge of Blade Runner. So emotionally stirring was Hauer’s speech that several crew members applauded him on set, leaving some even in tears. Later, in an interview with Dan Jolin that features in The Ridley Scott Encyclopedia, Hauer explained that his character’s monologue was necessary to “make his mark on existence … the replicant in the final scene, by dying, shows Deckard what a real man is made of”. Even putting the rich dialogue to one side, the composition of the shot and sequence is also marvellous, with the blue hue of Scott’s cinematography along with the imperfect structure of the surroundings helping to ground this story of androids and architects in a believable reality. Allowing Hauer to take centre stage, the music withdraws, and the pace slows, with the pitter-patter of rain heralding the slow, meditative death of Batty. https://faroutmagazine.co.uk/blade-runner-masterful-tears-in-the-rain-monologue/
item.dc:creator: Jbelkin
